content: Curiosity-driven autonomous agents can dynamically prioritize which episodic experiences to retain, compress, and summarize for long-term memory by weighting their compression effort based on how much each new experience actually reduces the agent's prediction uncertainty, rather than compressing all experiences uniformly. This creates a self-tuning long-term memory system that grows efficiently alongside the agent's expanding knowledge.
source_topics: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
